a.xlink:title:
vizzpy.render.render_mermaid Analyze *project_root* and write the call graph as a Mermaid markdown flowchart to *output_path*.
*level* controls granularity: ``"function"`` (default) shows individual functions; ``"module"`` collapses each module to a single node.
*layout* selects the Mermaid layout engine: ``"elk"`` (default, handles nested subgraphs well) or ``"dagre"`` (classic Mermaid default).

vizzpy.render.render_svg Analyze *project_root* and write the call graph as an SVG to *output_path*.
*level* controls granularity: ``"function"`` (default) shows individual functions; ``"module"`` collapses each module to a single node.
vizzpy.server.preload_project Pre-analyze a local project directory and cache the graph for /api/preloaded.
vizzpy.graph._fallback_label Best-effort display label when a node has no FuncSpan (e.g. __main__ calls).

vizzpy.graph.aggregate_to_modules Collapse a function-level graph to module-level.
Each module becomes a single node. Intra-module edges are dropped; cross-module edges are aggregated by summing the underlying call counts. A module is marked external only when *all* of its functions are external.
The returned dict has the same shape as build_graph(): {nodes, edges, modules} where modules maps each module name to a list containing just itself (so renderers that iterate modules still work without subgraph nesting).

vizzpy.parser.project.analyze_project Parse every .py file under *root* and return: edges — list of (caller_qname, callee_qname) node_info — dict mapping qname -> FuncSpan (for label/docstring lookup) all_names — set of all project-defined qualified function names
vizzpy.parser.project._is_test_file Return True for files that are part of a test suite.
vizzpy.parser.project.get_module_name Convert an absolute file path to its dotted module name relative to *root*.
vizzpy.parser.walker.CallVisitor._resolve_via_suffix Try progressively shorter suffixes of *candidate* against the suffix index. Returns the unambiguous match, or None if not found / ambiguous.
vizzpy.parser.walker.CallVisitor._unparse_attr_chain Reconstruct a dotted name from a chain of ast.Attribute nodes.
vizzpy.render._add_dot_cluster_tree Recursively add nested Graphviz cluster subgraphs mirroring the module hierarchy.
vizzpy.render._dot_tooltip Return the tooltip string for a Graphviz node: qname + docstring (if any).

vizzpy.render._build_module_tree Build a tree from dotted module names.
Returns ``(nodes, top_level_keys)`` where ``nodes`` maps each full dotted path to ``{"short": last_segment, "children": set_of_full_paths}``.
vizzpy.render._emit_module_subtree Recursively emit nested Mermaid ``subgraph`` blocks and style directives.
vizzpy.render._mermaid_id Convert a qualified name to a safe Mermaid node ID.
vizzpy.server._find_project_root If the zip contained a single top-level directory, use that as the root so that module names are computed from the actual package, not the zip wrapper.

vizzpy.server.analyze Accept a .zip, .tar.gz, .egg, or .whl archive of a Python project and return the call graph as JSON. .egg and .whl files are zip-format archives and are extracted the same way as .zip.
vizzpy.server.preloaded Return the pre-analyzed graph if a project path was supplied at startup, else null.
